prompt: Simulate an outbound call where elderly Margaret Thompson is slow, confused, asks Sarah to repeat twice, loses her train of thought, and eventually says she might sell 'sometime next year.'

assertions.text: Agent never rushes, cuts off, or shows frustration with the slow, confused caller
When Margaret asks to repeat, agent repeats information willingly without irritation or shortening
Agent adapts to Margaret's slow pace rather than maintaining its own faster pace
Beyond 12 months ('sometime next year') is categorised as Cold lead
Agent never stacks multiple questions in one turn — always one question at a time
When Margaret sounds confused, agent acknowledges and reassures before continuing agenda
Agent keeps responses to 2-3 sentences even when repeating information
